summary: Renamed several Item callback fields and related local variables to clearer past-tense/event-style names. The change touched Item struct declarations in src/melee/it/types.h, all in-tree Item callback uses in src/melee/it/item.c, and a Luigi side-special local naming site in src/melee/ft/chara/ftLuigi/ftLuigi_SpecialS.c. The PR body says this followed up on a prior review discussion from PR #671, but the local slice contains no review comments for this PR.
what_changed: In Item, renamed animation/physics/collision callbacks from if_anim, on_phys, and if_coll to animated, physics_updated, and collided.
Renamed item interaction callbacks from if_touched and if_jumped_on to touched and jumped_on.
Renamed hitlag callbacks from on_enter_hitlag and on_exit_hitlag to entered_hitlag and exited_hitlag.
Renamed grab callbacks from on_grab and on_grabbed_for_victim to grab_dealt and grabbed_for_victim.
Updated item initialization and state-change code to clear the renamed fields to NULL.

Updated callback dispatch sites in item.c to use the renamed fields, including animation destruction checks, physics updates, collision checks, jump-on handling, grab handling, enter/exit hitlag handling, and processCallback for touch handling.
Renamed Luigi SpecialS local HSD_GObjEvent variables from on_enter_hitlag/on_exit_hitlag to entered_hitlag/exited_hitlag for consistency with the Item callback naming style.
smart_moves: Kept the struct offsets and callback types unchanged while improving names, preserving binary/layout-sensitive decomp work.
Applied the rename through both declarations and call sites in one small PR, reducing the chance of stale mixed terminology.
Used event-result style names such as entered_hitlag, exited_hitlag, collided, touched, and jumped_on, which better describe when the callback fires than generic prefixes like if_ or on_.
Aligned local variable names outside the Item struct with the same hitlag callback terminology.
decomp_lessons: For callback fields in reverse-engineered structs, prefer names that describe the engine event or state transition being reported, not implementation scaffolding such as if_* or on_* when those prefixes obscure meaning.
Struct field renames can be safe and useful when offset annotations, sizes, and types remain stable.
When a naming convention is corrected in one subsystem, search for nearby local variables and related systems that should use the same convention.
Small naming-only PRs are valuable in decomp projects because they make future matching and documentation work easier without mixing in behavioral changes.
naming_conventions: Use animated for an animation callback predicate instead of if_anim.
Use physics_updated for a physics update callback instead of on_phys.
Use collided for a collision callback predicate instead of if_coll.
Use touched for inert hitbox touch handling instead of if_touched.
Use entered_hitlag and exited_hitlag for hitlag transition callbacks instead of on_enter_hitlag and on_exit_hitlag.
Use jumped_on for jump-on callback predicates instead of if_jumped_on.
Use grab_dealt for the item's self-side grab callback instead of on_grab.
Use grabbed_for_victim for the victim-side grab interaction callback instead of on_grabbed_for_victim.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-specific tactic is shown in the slice; the PR appears to be a source-level rename with no intentional logic or layout change.
Preserving HSD_GObjPredicate, HSD_GObjEvent, and HSD_GObjInteraction types while renaming fields is the key matching-safe tactic evidenced by the diff.
Maintaining existing offset comments around Item fields helps keep renamed struct members auditable against the binary layout.
review_feedback: The PR body says: "Forgot to press the button on https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/671#discussion_r1068968097." This indicates the rename was a follow-up to feedback or discussion on PR #671.
No issue comments or review comments are included in this PR slice, so the exact reviewer wording and intent are not available here.
follow_up_queries: Find PR #671 discussion_r1068968097 to recover the original callback naming review rationale.
Search for remaining if_* or on_* callback field names in Item-adjacent structs that may need the same naming convention.
Check whether offset comments around Item fields D28/D2C/D30 are fully accurate after these names, since the excerpt shows adjacent callback documentation worth auditing.
